comment: Shared verification vectors for every platform. The desktop smoke check sharedVectors runs all of these through the real implementations; iOS unit tests consume the identical file. Add cases here so both platforms prove the same behavior.

diff.name: phrase-level mishearing comes out as one pair
single-word capitalization tweak is style, not a correction

cases.name: case-insensitive whole phrase, punctuation-adjacent
cases.text: send it to My Email, thanks

chatGuard.name: live-observed 2026-07-22: silence artifact invited a chat reply
chatGuard.output: There is no text to clean up. Please provide the dictated speech to be converted into written text.

chatGuard.name: live-observed 2026-07-20: instruction-echo preamble
chatGuard.input: so um the budget is fine
chatGuard.output: Before sending the cleaned text, I will confirm it with you to ensure it meets the requirements. Please provide the dictated speech.
chatGuard.expected: so um the budget is fine

chatGuard.name: chatty answer to a polite transcript
chatGuard.output: You're welcome! Let me know if you need anything else.

chatGuard.name: two-word chat reply caught by the overlap floor
spoken punctuation command survives the one-word rule
digit conversion survives the one-word rule
a question is formatted, never answered
empty output fails open to the transcript

silenceSegments.name: mixed take keeps the real segment only
whispered speech stays under the threshold and survives
endpoint without segments falls through to plain text
